result: F-HOLO-8 (MIXED): regional 2nd-order pooling closes the generic-MLP(0.661)->oracle(0.935) gap (block-Laplacian 0.929, block-entropy 0.858) => the framework's ARCHITECTURAL PRINCIPLE (regional 2nd-order pooling) is the lever. BUT generic Laplacian (0.929) BEATS the framework entropy op (0.858) => NOT holonomy-specific; any 2nd-order regional bias suffices.

NEW_MECHANISM: regional 2nd-order pooling closes the gap a generic learner cannot
NEGATIVE_RESULT_REQUIRING_A_GUARD: framework's SPECIFIC entropy op is NOT better than generic Laplacian -> do not claim holonomy-specific capability; justify concentric build by a task needing the specific structure
novelty_classification.note: Third consistent instance (with F-HOLO-2, real-spine signed-link): framework PRINCIPLES validate, SPECIFIC-op superiority does not.

components_added_or_updated: curvature_field += block_entropy_features (framework 2nd-order pooling front-end) + block_laplacian_features (generic control)
guards_added: justify concentric Gomb-Soma by a task where the framework's SPECIFIC structure is necessary, not merely 2nd-order pooling (conv/Laplacian substitutes)

source_changes: examples/bias_discrimination.rs (NEW, TrainCfg struct for the trainer)

gates.bias_discrimination: regional 2nd-order pooling CLOSES the gap (0.66->0.93); NOT holonomy-specific (Laplacian 0.929 > entropy 0.858)
gates.lint: clippy -D warnings clean (8-arg trainer -> TrainCfg struct, no allow); fmt clean

integrity_note: Same data across arms; the generic MLP got a strong attempt (128/600/700); the two block arms share the same head hyperparameters (fair bias comparison). The holonomy-specificity NEGATIVE is not suppressed - the control beat the framework op and it is reported as the headline.
honest_scope: one task; conv baseline deferred; block front-ends use uniform column blocks. Consistent with the broader pattern (principles validate, specific ops do not beat generic baselines).
next_experiment_authorized: a task where the framework's SPECIFIC structure (non-abelian holonomy / signed-hypergraph) is NECESSARY and a conv/Laplacian cannot substitute - the real open problem gating the concentric build and any capability claim beyond 'regional 2nd-order pooling'.
next_experiment_proposal: design a task whose discriminative signal is genuinely NON-ABELIAN (order-of-loop matters; a scalar roughness / Laplacian cannot capture it) - e.g. classify by the NON-COMMUTATIVITY of regional holonomies (does the order of traversal change the outcome), which a 2nd-order scalar pooler is blind to but rotor_holonomy captures. If the framework op wins THERE and Laplacian/conv cannot, holonomy-specificity is finally demonstrated.
